item.title: great job, small note
item.content:encoded: love this, Anomalocaris is my fave animal and this was an excellent entry overall. One small thing, it mentions carapace on the body being replace by metal, but the only carapace on an Anomalocaris canadensis would be on the head, not the body

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Got a few proposals. First off, the reference to the Clever Girls can be made into a link to their MTF hub. Additionally, this article is featured in quite a string of tales from the Character Tournament from back in the day, starting with Trilobutts: Many Reasons To Kill Them, and 0 Reasons Not To. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

Reports of explosions and voices swearing about trilobites in Lake Ontario surfaced over the following days, prompting Foundation investigation. Mobile Task Force Nu-3 ("Limnophobia") and Mobile Task Force Phi-2 ("Clever Girls") were dispatched, quickly encountering the organism through the dispersal of trilobite-shaped monitoring buoys. Upon encountering SCP-4131 on 25-September-2015, the task forces announced themselves as a "trilobite extermination agency" that was looking to recruit the anomaly. SCP-4131 willingly entered containment soon after.

To this:

Reports of explosions and voices swearing about trilobites in Lake Ontario surfaced over the following days, prompting Foundation investigation. Mobile Task Force Nu-3 ("Limnophobia") and Mobile Task Force Phi-2 ("Clever Girls") were dispatched, quickly encountering the organism through the dispersal of trilobite-shaped monitoring buoys. Upon encountering SCP-4131 on 25-September-2015, the task forces announced themselves as a "trilobite extermination agency" that was looking to recruit the anomaly. SCP-4131 willingly entered containment soon after.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The organism is 1.6m long, abnormally larger than the majority of fossilized and living Anomalocaris specimens

I love that the Foundation just casually notes the existence of a living population of Anomalocaris.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m so glad the OCT contest exists because it introduced me to this work of art. I love the interview and the semi-naive personality of this one.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomolous favourite animal, explosives and trilobites (uh, trilobites getting blown up, I mean…)

+1.

(And, in fairness, after being… in suspended animation? Awake…? All that time, it might not be all that surprise 4131 is a little balmy. Assuming he/she was ever not in the first case…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! It definitely isn't perfect but for what's basically an attempt at pushing myself outside of my comfort zone I think it's turned out very well, so it's great to hear it's enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the interview had a couple slips, but they're super minor and come down to my own personal taste more than anything else. and i think you probably could have wrapped this up with a better and more impactful stinger, but what we have more than makes up for it. the imagery is good, there's little to no dead space, it hints at a story without mashing it in our face, the dialogue on the whole is fun and enjoyable, and your characterization is simple, but purposeful and efficient.

the article accomplishes its mission and doesn't overstay its welcome, and the mainlist would be worse off without it. this is great work. well done. ♥

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes, all you need is a little more gun.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Echoing Mortos.

While the description has enough of a weird factor to peak my interest, the interview fully seals the deal with the circular rambling of 4131. Hilarious!

+1. Well done, 9Volt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ sorta spoiler I guess??
- sorta spoiler I guess??

Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so did he confirm weapon wielding Raptors?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crap, sorry about that. >_> I just changed the image to a version that's roughly half the amount of bytes, is it any better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author, the image is 1.02 MB, which is kind of excessive for something that's about 300 px wide. Any chance you can shrink that down, for the people viewing your page on mobile with limited data plans?

If you would like assistance making the image smaller, I can help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly upvoted as soon as I the picture, because an anomalous anomalocaris is inherently something I feel this site should have.

And then the rest of the article really captured overall feel of Anomalocaris; it's a really weird and goofy looking critter that is also the earliest known apex predator, but probably a good portion of the reason why Trilobites had to evolve hard shells. So here we have one really embraces that dichotomy of silly and dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview here really captures the just obsessive rambling personality of this thing. Just a lot of weird fun. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First non-contest Series V article let's go.

Thanks to Countless Skies, Captain Kirby, Weryllium, PeppersGhost, MaliceAforethought, Mortos, Stallmantic, stormbreath for crit!

Image source:

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Anomalocaris_canadensis_-_reconstruction_-_MUSE.jpg
